channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: >SPCX > total crypto market cap >Elon Musk net worth >= BTC market cap imagine still bagholding fake internet money in y… — 16 replies — 64.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>SPCX > total crypto market cap >Elon Musk net worth >= BTC market cap imagine still bagholding fake internet money in y…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-17 12:34 UTC

OP

>SPCX > total crypto market cap
>Elon Musk net worth >= BTC market cap
imagine still bagholding fake internet money in year 2000+26

Early replies (first 3)

62374366
It's over for Epstein pedo coin and the entire crapto world.

62374368
>>62374364
Crypto bros what happened?... its 2026 and we still cant keep up with random single ticker...

62374370
>>62374368
ill tell you what happened.

you know all the people telling you to 'hodl'? well they dumped on your ass and bought lambos and apartments in dubai.

kek

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62374402
>>62374364
An illiquid 4% vc shitcoin is higher than the totally trumped by cz shitniggercoin mqrket? Wow, we are in high league now, when 90% drop in every single asset class?

62374406
>>62374364
>everyone on /biz/ smug posting
the top is literally in

62374485
>>62374364
>negative 4 billion Q1
Yeah I'm sticking to my gold miners. Enjoy your Ponzi I guess.

62374508
>>62374364
Your 9000 gazillion market cap is made through 4% (75B$ at its IPO price) of the floating... Meaning you only have to pump out the measly 4%/75B to make the remaining 96% pump by as much, without needing the money to do it.
Even the internet tulip money is less fake and gay than this shit

62374567
I got out of crypto a few years ago
it was fun for a while, but looking back it's incredibly embarrassing because it never actually did what it was supposed to do
when bitcoin exploded transaction fees and times made it absolutely fucking useless
and all of the cheap and low transaction alternatives are insecure, buggy, and dogshit

62374825
>>62374364
>Elon is back
>WE are back

62374839
>>62374567
Look, the only retard that didn't make money on 2021 lmao

62374878
>>62374839
yea I made money on stocks instead, better than scam digital tokens l0l

62374901
Spacex holds bitcoin

62374972
>>62374364
imagine he's getting a stroke, would be almost black swan event for the market at this point.

62374992
>>62374972
>he doesn't know the board has body doubles in preparation for this scenario

62375392
My cope is part of his nw is in crypto

62379231
>>62374878
Nobody did a x10/x20 with stocks in just a few months, get the fuck out

item.title: "He didn't buy SPCX" edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: >Gambling: >Pre-Market and… — 89 replies — 59.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
"He didn't buy SPCX" edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: >Gambling: >Pre-Market and…

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-06-17 11:11 UTC

OP

"He didn't buy SPCX" edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars:
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>Previous >>62372298

Early replies (first 3)

62373348
I am still making money off of sneedisk
>should've bought more than one SPCX edition
thank you for your attention to this matter

62373354
>>62373348
why so edgy

62373362
looks like it's headed back up after that 230 le correction

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62375597
SpaceX is a sub 25 dollar a share stock

62375604
>>62373337
>me sitting watching the spcx tape live, finger cocked and right to smash that put button at the first sign of weakness

62375610
SpaceX is a 500 dollar company

62375614
its doing it again...

62375676
>>62375614
>its doing it ag-ACK!

62375680
SPCX and KMB are the only green in my portfolio

62375685
>>62375680
cost average?

62375689
I just made $1 on SPCX
I LOVE ELON MUSK

62375690
>>62375107
Good. Somebody needs to buy my bags one day. Stay out.

62375745
algos arent letting anything run today
if somethign significant supposed to be happening or is it just warch fears, which are supposed to be tomorrow

62375758
Nobody is making money off of these oil prices. The only guy making any money off of the changes in price of oil is Baron Trump boss.

62375764
>>62375685
All my shares in SPCX are IPO at $135 and my KMB is at $98.

62375767
>>62375595
Are you talking about SpaceX? Because it more than any other can exactly do that.
This is exactly what the 4% floating is designed for.
This 4% was only 75B at its IPO. You only need to pump that to suddenly make the other remaining 96% pump by as much in %, and thus get le meme multi-trillion valuation and turn it into gazillion valuation.
It's all fucking FAKE and GAY.

62375800
LOL

62375814
>>62375767
>only 75B
what do you mean only?
that's like 10 gamestops, I agree with anon, if retail could gamma squeeze $75b of equities then half of the russel would be squeezing daily

62375840
>>62375800
>June 15th
tweet doesn't exist
https://xcancel.com/AP

62375897
everything is on a uptrend despite the algo interference

62375982
>>62375897
ok i guess i meant just SPCX LOL

62376054
fuck tech and fuck AI, pls keep poisoning the market space bros
t. tech worker

62376066
>>62376054
>he doesn't know spacex is an AI company

62376076
>>62376066
in space tho so it's cooler

62376311
i think that Greenshoe Option is fucking with the nav

62376602
>>62373337
I only managed to snag £5700 worth of shares our of £20k.
Still, better than a kick up the arse.

62376628
Most retail couldn't get shares anyway.

62376667
I'm about to buy 44,000 dollars worth of shares but am scared. Convince me to do it.

62376709
>>62376667
do it bro dont be a pussy youre gonna miss out

62377887
looks like the reason for the downturn was the acquisition of another AI company called cursor
tomorrow might be a good run
funny how i didnt even think to check the spcx specific news during the day

62378018
Just stop doing things, have lockheeb and raytheon remotely turn off all israeli weapons, have congress freeze any and all mergers and buyouts, take away trump's phone, double elon's ketamine dose
All i ask for is one month of no news, nothing, complete radio silence

62379060
>>62373337
>"He didn't buy SPCX"
SPCX was easiest buy&dump get 10-30% rich in 1 day trick in the last 4 weeks. Imagine missing it, lol

62379081
Why is there so much fud over gold and silver miners lately? Do they really think that foreign nations are going to stop buying gold after the Iran and Greenland fiascos? Do they really think the fed is going to turn off the money printer? Do they really think you can have an ai movement with a silver shortage?

Is it an IQ thing?

item.title: >month 8 at my first office job >havent touched more than 33% of my salary since i started according to chatgpt i can re… — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>month 8 at my first office job >havent touched more than 33% of my salary since i started according to chatgpt i can re…

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-17 12:40 UTC

OP

>month 8 at my first office job
>havent touched more than 33% of my salary since i started
according to chatgpt i can retire in 8 years at this rate
there HAS to be a faster way to escape this daily humiliation ritual
i literally have not felt joy since i started working

Early replies (first 3)

62377269
>>62377212
8 years and you're complaining?

also this isn't your blog, kys nigger

62377302
>>62377212
what do you do and what's your salary? maybe i can help

62377309
>>62377212
Stop working in an office and go clean a portojohn.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62377313
if it makes you feel better, 2026 has been the worst year for layoffs since 2022.

62377334
>>62377302
IT jeet
it was about 2.5k dolla a month but now its more like 4k a month
>>62377313
if i got laid off id be happy

62377336
>>62377334
Don’t listen to chat gpt about what to do. Go to a real financial advisor at a reputable firm and ask them how to retire in 5 years.

62377341
>>62377334
I take it you're a literal jeet. Because if you make 4k and only touch 1/3 of that you must be living in a literal shithole.

>t. Europoor making 5k eurobucks net and that's considered a comical high salary as an IT jeet here

62377347
>>62377336
i dont wanna pay money for someone to be laughed at
>>62377341
i live in a country with low cost of living + im extremely frugal

62377359
>>62377347
It's okay to say a 3rd world country anon.
I'm not gonna call you nigger lmao.
To your topic we're on the same bost., I'll have to continue wage cucking 10years + at this rhythm to be able to make it

62378609
Any form of working is torture to me, how do I explain the feeling to normies.

62379217
>>62378609
just tell them
>I have the mind of a slave
and they'll understand

62379250
>>62377313
we can't all be doing well otherwise inflation would be 2-3x worse

it sucks but what can you do

item.title: >got liquidated on 7x BTC leverage day trading >quit his youtube channel and disappeared from the internet — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>got liquidated on 7x BTC leverage day trading >quit his youtube channel and disappeared from the internet

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-17 12:30 UTC

OP

>got liquidated on 7x BTC leverage day trading
>quit his youtube channel and disappeared from the internet

Early replies (first 3)

62379094
I hate to be that guy, but source?

62379099
>>62379094
he was a tech worker that got laid off because big tech has no incentive paying you $300k+ for prompting an AI harness just because "you know how to work it and be an architect" and just pass on most of that earnings to some landlord.

62379103
>>62379099
I asked for source and got emotional comment...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62379107
>>62379103
you're retarded. there you go

62379110
>>62379103
it's true my nigger. what incentive does a company have to pay you when there's 10,000 in line to do the same shit for far less money across the world. WFH was intentionally made for cost of living arbitrage. go into trades school to be a tech at a real datacenter to maintain the hardware; god this planet is fucked.

62379111
>>62379107
The OP literally stated he got liquidated on Bitcoin day trading.
I don't care about your feelings about this YouTuber, fuck off.

62379113
>>62379110
>cost of living arbitrage
QRD?

62379118
This is the most schizo responses I have ever seen, I'm out.

62379120
>>62379113
>you're a "talented tech worker saar"
>you demand $400k to live in SF and pay some jewish landlord $5k in rent to live in a closet
>a company must choose to pay you, labor that is oversupplied and not returning much in investment or choose a substitute that performs relatively equivalently for far less money because his rent is cheaper and he will live like a king on $90k
people think it's all just a charity and that HR wants to give them a job because they're good at what they do. that's not fucking enough, you need to compete on cost. you dont have that luxury of demanding a high salary when... actually yeah there is plenty of others that will do what you do for less. universities will not tell you this.

62379197
>>62379088

His shitcoin was fun to watch, I hold some million token myself.

If someone wanted to make money on a low volume shitcoin, there you go.

62379222
The replies here are very brown coded

item.title: >he went all in on everything except the only investment that matters Why didn't YOU invest in your family? — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>he went all in on everything except the only investment that matters Why didn't YOU invest in your family?

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-17 12:40 UTC

OP

>he went all in on everything except the only investment that matters

Why didn't YOU invest in your family?

Early replies (first 3)

62379163
you're asking why I didn't invest in my family...

and I will ask in return...

Is killing niggers free and open-source?

62379166
I personally view having kids an essential life goal.
Just give me more time.

62379173
>>62379160
I'm 29 with $300k I'm gonna get my gf pregnant at 30. Hopefully I'll have 400k by the

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62379191
>>62379160
I'm trying, bro.

62379204
>>62379166
>I just need to complete this massive list of things that likely won't happen before I start a family

the equivalent of waiting for a big price dip in a good stock that you subconsciously know isn't going to happen

62379219
>>62379160
Bad ROI and infinite P/E.

62379223
>>62379204
Having kids is like being a cuck in a relationship. You watch from the sidelines as others fullfill their lives while you wait your sloppy seconds.

62379248
>>62379223
Lol

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 52 replies — 28.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-06-17 10:53 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62374471
>>62373752
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62374533
anyone wants to learn about photonics? some stocks in that area have gone ballistic, i'm talking 10000% returns in 4 years

62374614
>>62373752
$8k nw fag reporting in

I bought spacex at ATH so I'm doing to 3-fig nw pretty soon wish me luck

62374764
>>62374614
whats the thinking process behing going all in in spcx? i am just curious

62374781
>>62373752
Based /x/ anon, keep fighting the good fight, and if you get something going please fucking pump my Cheese bags.

62374835
>4 figure club thread
>half the posts are animeposters
Many such cases

62374867
>>62373798
Mah nigga this is just Gabriel-chan. She's a neet that didn't do shit about her angelic job, her demon friends started helping her out. Yes, she will be a perfect base for our mascot.

62374886
>>62373888
Based trips.

>Next order of business.
>$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$

The way i see it, you guys need to learn how to DCA. That is easy, just buy whatever you want on monthly basis. The thing that we need to understand is: which ones to buy? Well I find that /smg/ threads are great for that.

>But we're poorfags who cant even invest 100 bucks on something
Yea, me too son, me too

62375712
>>62373752
how to make money as a poorfag /biz/raeli guide:
>prolific ai shit. bery klaustrophian
>work a wage job
>lie cheat and steal; have no remorse about it
>a penny saved is a penny earned. literally.
>unlearn your poorfag tendencies and your scarcity mindset trauma
>steal from homeless people
>street busking on off days
put everything on the stock market. fuck crypto.
>don't cheap out on food though. good nutrition makes for better decisions
>if ur on meds, start selling them
>dont be an autist who views the law as an unbreakable fact of life.
>get into options trading if you can. read the options playbook
>stfu about your money adventures
>SHUT THE FUCK UP ABOUT MONEY
>DO NOT TELL ANY1 IRL ABOUT WHAT YOU'RE DOING

62375724
>>62374533
QUBT,RGTI,IONQ

i had cheap entry prices but sold too early. now i want to kill myself

62376678
Bump, will be off work soon

62376699
>>62373803
Not sure if you noticed but you are engaging with bots in a bot thread

62376812
>>62376678
Still wagecucking, we left off at figuring out how to profit from making art. Want me to create a thread on /ic/ to invite drawfags?

62376829
>>62373772
Which eggregore is most predatory to us poor fags? Have our new eggregore waifu kill them

62376867
>>62376829
That's easy, it's called the grabbler.
https://youtu.be/Q4PC8Luqiws?si=wQBcUv8QOcxDJAXE

62377559
>>62376812
Drawfag days are numbered
They are gay, over-socialized and love the status quo
You would probably have better results recruiting AI "slop" artists from /g/
Despite what the sour grapes gatekeeping "artists" say, AI people put a lot of thought effort in their craft

62377563
>>62374193
BBWI

62377801
Okay guys


Here's what I have:

1. We scour the archive for leaks, gems and potential moon shots. It will be akin to searching through the dump for retro consoles. I looked through the archives and saw like 10 people invested into Palantir before it was big so I'm not too hopeful on /biz/ being the place for breadcrumbs but you never know. LINK was shilled early here.

2. We work together to look at certain patterns through events in the last 20 years. Let's not pretend reality isn't scripted. For instance! If you believed that SPACEX would rise BECAUSE Elon already had some gay scripted event with the SEC and TSLA back in '17 or '18. You would've made good money. No need for fundamentals. Just schizo and occult vibes.

3. We work on building better resumes. Lie. Lie. Lie. We find the best WFH jobs, or jobs that would NEVER hire us and "modify" our resumes to look better. Personally I hate waging and this would remind me of high school wage preparatory classes. Also this is kind of Jeety and interferes with my Weltanschauung.

4. The Drawfags idea as per
>>62373902

He will have to tell us what this Idea is and NO we are not leaving /biz/


5. The TCG pokemon way, as per >>62374140 Not a bad idea seeing as there is already a TCG general. We will have to be willing to dirty our hands with pokeniggers. If it ends poorly then maybe we can impress 18-19 art hoes with our cards.

6. We can also learn Photonics as per >>62374533 Rigorous study, I would purchase the udemy course and we all hit the books and discuss daily who has the best light tech. Probably will be boring.

Gentleman, please contribute. We need all our finest minds

GET THE FUCK OUT IF YOU HAVE MORE THAN $10,000

>>62374471
>>62374086
>>62373881

62377818
>>62377801
Also we want to pick something and stick with it. At least for 30 days to 3 months. If it isn't working then it isn't working. I don't see why ALL of us can't make it to $10k. It would also be best if we pick a route that if it doesn't work out then we at least learned something. When you invest in a shit coin and it 200x and goes to $0, what did you learn compared to someone who started an indoor shrimp farming who had a good run but had to declare bankruptcy? What REAL WORLD skills were obtained? Nothing?

62377841
>>62374042

62377846
We'll all be rich!

62377858
/biz/ is slow these days, I will check back in an hour. More suggestions would be lovely. Then we unanimously agree on something or maybe just roll for it in true 4chan fashion.

62377867
>>62377801
>He will have to tell us what this Idea is and NO we are not leaving /biz/
I am going to slowly invite drawfags from /ic/ to get at least one of them to collaborate with us and figure out how to sell art whether it be through patreon, crypto, whatever means to make a nickel. I only know of two, one of them is just beginning to draw again and doodles the cutest bears.
>>62377858
I'd like to believe a lot of us wagecuck and simply do not have the time of day but we'll make something happen.

62377880
7. We scour /smg/ as per >>62374886 and report back to this thread with the underrated, most talked about and genuinely good picks. Since there are multiple /smg/ threads this would be great.

NOTE: We should just use the current /smg/ threads and the archived ones at least within the past 1-2 years. So if you know a stock that everyone is talking about on reddit, please die. Reddit is not /smg/. Our pool that we search through will be /smg/. It would be best as it would help narrow our search and some people on /smg/ really do know their stuff. There is an individual there bragging daily about how he told everyone to buy Intel.

I am open to criticism and suggestions

62377886
>>62373752
If you actually want to get out of five figures your best bet is a six figure salary. I'm not joking, invest in yourselves.

62377896
if you have earned income and don’t have a retirement account, I would open a Roth IRA in addition to a regular taxable investment account. get in the habit of making regular contributions to both accounts. automatic deposits would be best if possible. even if you don’t make a lot, getting in the habit of investing is worthwhile. it’s best to invest in an etf like VTI or an s&p 500 etf. if there are dividends, set up automatic dividend reinvestment. (If you have a taxable account you’ll still need to pay tax on dividends, even if you reinvest them).

62378002
>>62373918
Well the /ic/ thread got deleted within minutes this is why I am cautious whenever we start to plan or congregate together its like they hate that.

62378439
>>62374533
Photonics sounds solid
I base all of the following off my schizo intuition:

1. I keep hearing about how Elon musk is a retard and SpaceX IPO is a rug pull. But you don't get that rich by being stupid.

2. I can't see X ever being profitable (though it is valuable for other reasons), and nobody gives a shit about electric cars anymore. But SpaceX... he is throwing money on that for a reason.

3. And Starlink is the world's biggest photonics deployment in space right now.
Satellite internet + photonics is going to be a huge deal
- cheap, super fast internet (terabits per second) that doesnt depend on today's bloated over-regulated infrastructure, and you can access it from anywhere
- Governments - specially EUcucks - keep making it difficult for you to use the internet and have free speech, could get BTFO the same way as the twitter trannies did when elon bought X
- Any retard shithole country with underwater drones can cut underwater internet cables, shooting down a satelite would be more difficult
- Perfect opportunity for a psyop to push people into using starlink instead of traditional intenet

If you couple that with AI companies also wanting photonics for their datacenters, you could get a lot of money this way

62378965
Brothers,
Just learn how to use the order book in polymarket, it's full of idiots that are willing to buy your bags for absurd prices. We can set our profile pictures to anime pic rel >62373866 to coordinate, target events like the current fifa event and mass shill or fud a team or group of teams so we can pump our bags
Two days ago i made 4K by just selling Belgium to win the world cup at 3x the actual price and some nigger actually bought it

62379061
>>62377886
Vague platitude

Option number 8 by >>62378965

There could be gains to be made on polymarket. Many of us are American so we'd either use kalshi or a vpn

The anon was so kind to give us an excerpt on his success and /biz/ has yet to make an impact on these markets like it did in 2021 in the BSC casino

By the end of the day, we shall have a decision set

item.title: Questions from a Mid-20’s Investor — 45 replies — 21.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Questions from a Mid-20’s Investor

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-06-17 10:33 UTC

OP

Hello /biz/ I will try and keep this brief
Currently I have been investing most of my take home pay simply into VOO as I only just recently started investing
It seemed simple, solid mid-long term option, and sensical for starting out to not fuck up while getting exposure
Now, this has nothing to do with FOMO, but I’m wondering if I should start aiming towards investing in either specific sector ETFs or maybe specific stocks to chase higher returns
My portfolio is currently at $60K mostly in VOO, perhaps with enough DCA I can reach $100k by years end or at least close to that, depends on growth
Currently my total portfolio return ranges from 17-20% depending on the month for context
I’ve seen recently how well things like SOXX have been doing and was considering finding a sector ETF or two to go into since I already have so much broad market exposure
To be clear, this is not me chasing returns based off past performance, I am well aware growth has to come from somewhere and is not infinite so I’m considering CAGRs as far back as possible
With all this in mind, any recommendations? Find a good sector ETF, go into a specific stock, or maybe just focus on getting more income first and then continue to just VOO and chill
And no I don’t wanna sell options doing covered calls or buy your shit coin etc etc
I should add the sectors I’m interested in are semi-conductors, energy (ones that deal with technology needed to cool data centers), and perhaps drones / nuclear for commercial and military usage

Early replies (first 3)

62369867
>>62369835
XLK
SOXL

62369880
>>62369835
Recreate the ETF so you avoid paying management fees.. Not super relevant for VOO, but if you go into niche sector specific ETFs, the management fees are usually over 0.5%

62370000
>>62369867
Not sure how I feel about the triple leverage with SOXL can you red pill me on it, it’s the same deal as QQQ vs TQQQ or whatever the hell it’s called right
But otherwise you’re just advising do some tech and semiconductor either way
>>62369880
Fair point I’ll consider that

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62371007
>>62370996
You're financially ok with brown nipples?

62371211
>>62371007
A lot are pink
Honestly I’ve never seen a brown nipple before

62371239
>>62371211
pink nip /= nip
anime is not real life chud.
jap girls have brown ones irl

62371250
>>62369835
Honestly man, the amount of effort it takes to beat the SP500 (or NASDAQ, which is even a bit better recently and imo long term from now on) is relatively high. You see countless examples of lifelong financial/investing experts who would have done better if they just did nothing but buy the SP500 and sit on it.

I'm invested more heavily into semiconductors and AI stocks but that's because I have industry knowledge. Industry knowledge can help you determine what is and is not good for long term investing. But then again, I also have industry knowledge in medicine and I won't touch biotech/pharma stocks with a ten foot pole.

62371385
>>62371250
Yeah I was considering just going into QQQ and accepting more ups and downs
I don’t know if it’s the best comparison but comparing it’s CAGR to VOO it has either outperformed or tied with VOO recently
Obviously the down years are way worse but again that’s why I am using CAGR as my comparison basis
It seems like this AI shit isn’t going away any time soon leading to something so focused on the top of the market like QQQ seeming more enticing

62371396
>>62369835
Basically I want to be your boyfriend or just glorified cockcleaner while both of our ports build up to 100k (but mine faster than yours but that's okay)

62371737
Also my portfolio currently sits at $61,000
I have a goal of reaching 100k by year end
There’s 174 week days left in the year, ignoring market closed due to holiday
100,000 - 61,000 = 39,000
39,000 / 174 = 224
224 / 61,000 = .003
So
If my math is right
My account only needs to grow .3% per market day to reach 100,000
61,000 * 1.003^174 = 102,728
Am I correct here

62371763
>>62371385
>It seems like this AI shit isn’t going away any time
It's not, this shit is going to fundamentally change most industries in a way that most people cannot even understand. Its primary limitation is just that older generations can't adapt to it, which means it has naysayers now and a clear path of progressive growth over time, similar to computing decades ago.

It's not just AI though honestly. These companies are the future. With population growth leveling out worldwide, there's not going to be a sudden expansion of demand for food, for minerals, for many traditional industries. NASDAQ is better primed for investment in future tech, hence why I prefer it. Or tech ETFs like VGT, but those are less diversified. Just sinking 50/50 into SP500 and NASDAQ might be fine for you to let it ride

62371848
>>62370048
SEX

62372037
>>62371763
i see, thanks for the input

62372074
>>62371737
Heyyy we're port buddies :) YAGMI

62372118
>>62371737
also this fails to recognize buying more shares bringing that rate down significantly

62372136
>>62372074
>>62371737
I lied my port's now at 77k LOL YANGMI

62372167
Due to volatility and human nature its actually mentally easier to go into 5 companies you believe in. keep most money in S&P and add 5

62372242
>>62372136

62373602
Do we got a chance for 100k by years end fellas

62373605

62373753
>>62373602

62374176

asphyxiated to death by her big milkies mmmm
yes

62374196

shut the frick up fakecel

62374412
>>62373753

62374944
>>62373602

62377984
>>62374944

62377988

62377998

62378015

62378019

62378356

item.title: PSA for SpaceX baggies — 29 replies — 12.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
PSA for SpaceX baggies

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-06-17 10:18 UTC

OP

Reminder:
The moon is made of poison.
Unfathomably small shards of jagged micro-glass that have never has their razor edges dulled by wind or rain that eat through Kevlar space suit like abrasive fluid.

It gets everywhere, and if you accidently inhaled a cloud of it, you will die, either shortly, or later due to its carcinogenic chemical properties (its also radioactive in the extreme).

Neither the moon or the mars have a living core.
This means:
>A) Anything of value like gold or silver or tantalum or palladium sank to the core a billion years ago
>B) Any atmosphere you terraform with nukes (retarded) would blow away. (Obviously, you cannot re-light a planets core, if you have that level of energy, you can just open a wormhole to another earth.)

Asteroid mining is a meme.
You cannot mine asteroids anymore today with Falcon 9 than you could 40 years ago.

Roids (with heavy metals) are protoplanets, like mars and the moon their cores are dead, and that is where all the heavy rare earths are.

You cant just land Curiosity on the surface and have it sweep up the gold and iridium into a bin.

So you're either drilling to the core in space autonomously through a km of rock and iron hoping a gas pocket doesn't blow (not possible on earth autonomously), or you're crashing one here.

In either case, you need the fuel to get a skyscrapers weight in robotic mining equpment to the roid to drill a km deep, or you need the fuel to move a skyscraper.This would be more fuel than has ever been launched, and you'd need fuel to get that fuel up there.

Won't waste time on datacenter memes, theres a 4 second delay to the moon and back, on top of normal internet speed delays, and tons of noise.
If you want to spend 3x the gdp of europe building dial up on the moon be my guest.

Hope this helps.
Hope you don't lose your shit due to unintentional ignorance.
If you do now, its willful ignorance.

Early replies (first 3)

62377346
>>62377342
cope

62377349
>>62377342
https://science.nasa.gov/science-research/science-enabling-technology/technology-highlights/new-moon-duster-will-help-clean-nasa-assets-in-space/

62377351
Asteroid mining is absolutely the future actually and I will be signing up to be a rockrat at the earliest opportunity.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62377356
>>62377342
I agree that cities on mars and the moon are a meme but we can have a science base there, there is valuable science to be done and I'd also like to point out that virtually all the apollo astronauts lived to their 80s.

62377361
The bean counters told me we literally could not afford to buy seven dollars worth of moon rocks, much less seventy million. Bought 'em anyway. Engineers said the moon rocks were too volatile to experiment on. Tested on 'em anyway. Ground 'em up, mixed 'em into a gel. And guess what? Ground up moon rocks are pure poison. I am deathly ill. Still, it turns out they're a great portal conductor.

62377364
Elon isn't going to the Moon, he's going to Mars. Which he is going to rename to "Planet X 420-69." SpaceX will make $1 trillion revenue per month from Planet X 420-69 mining operations.

62377370
You will never be a woman

62377374
>>62377361
He was a hero, truly. Pioneer spirit.

62377393
>>62377342
someone is pissed he missed out kek

62377394
>>62377351
A typical small 200-meter asteroid (not the kind that have anything of value, but I'm using this for effect) typically has a mass between 4 million to 10 million metric tons.

To change the velocity (the trajectory) of one by 1 (one) meter per second, you would need roughly 10 billion kilograms of rocket fuel using current best in class high efficiency motors.

SpaceX has lowered the cost per kilo to $3000ish.

7 × 109 kg × $3,000/kg = $21, 000, 0000, 000, 0000
>21 TRILLION DOLLARS
The GDP of America for a year.
to move a small roid by 1m/sec

In reality you would need to divert it by at a minimum 100m/sec on the absolute best case close orbital path, or the sum total of humanities GDP throughout the 20th century for a single roid.

Hope this makes it clear.
You're not mining roids.

62377400
>>62377394
>7 billion kg*, 10 is for the higher mass range, calculation is still correct

62377412
>>62377342
dr;ns
Bought more actually, kys fud nigger

62377445
>>62377394
Sorry you just failed your rock rat exam. You're going to be a potato farmer stuck on earth.

62377450
>>62377394
space x is worth 100 trillion dollars so it can move the rock easily

62377468
>>62377342
you are 100% correct, but SPCX and modern economy in general aren't about anything real. it's just a falshmob replacement for the debased currency. and much like national currency, it comes with a founding myth.

62377473
>>62377394
nothing serious can be done in space until we mastered nanotechnology which we ourselves are made of. at which point we can just wirehead in pure bliss forever and not bother doing anything(or, more likely we're killed by an AGI singleton which then proceeds to paperclip maximise its own version of bliss state forever)

62377480
>>62377394
You could just use nukes to redirect it
Donald will sell Irans nukes to Elon
Nukes can terraform mars also

Look I’m going to make it this time, for real I can feel it this is the one the way it happens no bogged shit can keep us from being set for life on something this big

62377483
>>62377480
baggie mindset
NGMI

62377491
>>62377349
We finna vacuum the moon

62377537
>>62377349
>gets inhaled
>circulates in blood
>nukes your balls

62377549
In theory we could seed Mars and the moon with extremophile bacteria and see if any survive.
Perhaps after a few million years they would evolve a complete biosphere for future humans to live on.

62377552
>>62377537
Just leave your suit outside.

62377918
>>62377549
Wouldn't work thoughbeit.

The core is dead, no plates or volcanism so nutrients don't get recycled.
Bacteria would consume whatever energetic chemical bonds are left on the surface that haven't been broken down by cosmic rays in a few generations and die off.

62377929
>>62377394
>roughly 10 billion kilograms of rocket fuel
You don't mount a rocket on the asteroid lol
You use concentrated sunlight to vaporise small patches of the surface to push the asteroid around
The DART mission showed that dumping energy into the surface of an asteroid creates a very effective jet effect, which was far more significant at moving the asteroid than the momentum of the impactor

62377932
>>62377918
>in a few generations
The resurfacing you're talking about is a concern on geological timescales. You could have a living Mars for thousands of years before you need to manually resurface it

62377941
>>62377552
Wojack is in the driver's seat. The moron getting out of the suit is an NPC.

62377957
>>62377342
That's cool and all... but the earth is flat and space is fake. Therefore "the moon" and "Mars" is just another domed area on our Earth and the Anti-Christ (Elon) will soon take people to Mars to colonize it while Christ comes back to rule from the Moon.

62378581
this thread is bullish for precious metals

62379006
>>62377932
>you could spend a trillion dollars to grow an invisible layer of extremophile bacteria living for 2000 years and then dying off
The desperation to make mars a thing is real.
Its not a thing.
It had its time, its just dead now,

If you want to argue venus...
that seems impossible if extremely extremely unlikely,
it certainly is for humans, but you might speculatively by able to create an organism that could survive those conditions,
venus does have turnover or energetic compounds bacteria could eat, heat, energy, movement, protection from radiation, weather to churn the soup, etc etc.

mars is impossible.
its dead.
a dead rock flying through space surrounded by the thinest vapors of an atmosphere, basically just a rock in a nebula in deep space.
there is no energy or movement or cycling there and you can't create something from nothing.

item.title: (srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb? — 38 replies — 11.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
(srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb?

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-06-17 09:16 UTC

OP

June 11: chop as market gets ready for pump on spacex trading day

June 12 - June 16: iran FUD is metabolized and market goes up on spacex


June 17 - June 22: FOMC on June 16 EOD causes fud, plus more iran fears

June 22 - Oct 10: NDQ floats upwards 40% while S&P chops and eventually goes up 10%

Oct 10: NDQ reaches a generational top - OpenAI IPOs - Fed announces more hikes due to AI labor inflation - republicans lose congress making trump a lame duck president = no more stimulus grift to pump markets
Oct 10, 2026 - ~Oct 10 2027: as a consequence of the lame duck presidency and the lack of stimulus and the selling of AI venture capital bags onto retail, S&P sells off 40% to round trip all the way back down to 5000

~Oct 10 2027: anthropic ipos

~Oct 10 2027 onwards: pretty much a new 20 year cycle begins and most likely the end of humanity as we birth sentience of machines.

Early replies (first 3)

62349969
you need to factor in the wc which is the biggest npc bread & circus event in modern history

62349972
>>62349960
i just matched up the timelines for 1973 and 2000 btw, that's where I'm getting the play by play for things. I reckon the trading AI's are probably averaging the two together given the macro environment

62349980
>>62349969
ah good point, ya so that might erase the june 17 - june 22 fud period and we go straight into june 22 - oct 10 period

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62355245
>>62349960
elf

62359675

62359749
>>62359675
what do you want to hear? the market will go up or go down. your doom pronouncement is stupid since people have been predicting the apocalypse for thousands of years and have always been wrong. also LLMs are gay and AI companies deserve to be buried in piracy and antitrust lawsuits by the next administration. I hope every AI ceo ends up in jail

62359780
>>62359708

62359931
>>62359749

7*4 = 28 and they get the satisfaction of destroying a holiday. Will also hand everything to the dems in the next election.

62359991
>>62349960

62359998
>>62359991

62361834

62361911
>>62349960

>FOMC -> NDQ goes up

62361941

62363423
>>62361911

https://www.cmegroup.com/markets/interest-rates/cme-fedwatch-tool.html

warsh is gonna come out next week and be dovish, but the market will act like its not simply because they're range trading, and they're gonna rebuy their positions.

62364277
motheryuckers, this snail post will exist for years

62364284
>>62349960
>metabolized
Stop reading right there.

62366455
>>62364284

don't worry, illiterate retard opinions aren't needed

62366592
>>62366455
I didn't give an opinion.

62368882
snailposting till nostradamus pomp or domp predictions

62370523
snailposting is just transient guys

62370546
>>62370523
Reddit elf

62370557
>>62370546
tard

62370592
>>62370523

How certain are you about that November election? Rs literally gerrymandered the south and killed D seats + added 12. Trump picks have won every primary solidifying his control. I think it will be a narrow rig in order for the country to not go "troubles" Dem or Rep doesn't fucking matter, probably Rep +1 in the house

62372434
>>62370592
hmm good point, but there's no way they can unfuck the public opinion by november. The pain of inflation will be too fresh, and the memory of Iran causing that pain to boot. All the poor people will be out in force

62372555
>>62359780
Is Frieren a virgin?

Thanks for this thread OP

62375431
>>62372555
i am a virgin, yes, i am frieren btw, you're welcome

62378224
i am goy cattle slave

anyway, let's see what warsh has to say tomorrow. I'm guessing it'll be exactly what everyone expects, and the market will dump, because "oh my god it's what we expected therefore we must domp eeet" and then news headlines will make up some bullshit about it not being exactly what everyone expected

then we'll start our final ascent next week

the alternative is as the other anon said and the WC just completely eats any remaining fud and we begin the ascent tomorrow.

idk call it

62378317
>>62349960
I'll play along; assuming you're 100% correct: how do I benefit the most from this timeline?

62378908
>>62378317

For the next leg, just buy a portfolio of ndq & btc, maybe hype too if you're close to arthur and have advanced notice of when their gang is gonna commence_ze_domp.exe. It'd be hard to pick a specific time + stock individually due to how many of them have been bid up already, I think it'll just be ndq as a whole that floats upwards carried by the larger ai stocks getting bid up on anthropic bullish biz announcements


At the top, take out far OTM 3-6 month puts on the stocks way out on the risk curve of AI. You can probably just ask claude or chatgpt to come up with a list, but this list was decent (not great, but ok):

https://www.reddit.com/r/wallstreetbets/comments/1tuwp73/in_the_ai_gold_rush_what_are_the_shovels/

close the puts once spy/ndq are around 15% off the top, nearing the "bear market" 20% fud line where the money printers tend to start spinning

At the 20% fud line, short vix futures


after the initial leg down, you can either assume gold/btc/commodities will do well (like they did in 1973) or continue to be suppressed with the market's decline (like they did in 2000). If they're suppressed, then just scalp spy on each retrace and short it at the top of each retrace (whatever vehicle works here). If they're not suppressed, I'd just buy gld/btc/commodities and capture long term cap gains and chill


just a footnote: the reason I say 3-6 months is because of trump, if he wasn't president, I'd just do 1-2 month. If you feel he won't pull some bullshit, then ya go for it

risk size is whatever you want, don't use this post to construct your reason for suicide pls

62378916
>>62349960
Problem with posts like these is they make the predictions way too fast. Put the 2027 prediction in 2037 and yeah sure.

Remember, "nothing ever happens"

62378940
>>62378317
also, the full retard method for June 22 - Oct 10 assuming im 100% correct would be spcx, but i have no insight to their insiders

item.title: wtf is this website — 5 replies — 6.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
wtf is this website

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-17 11:57 UTC

OP

yo, i think i found one of the weirdest websites i've seen in a while

stumbled across a site called zaum.app and i genuinely have no idea what i'm looking at

at first i thought it was some kind of anonymous message board because anybody can post messages without even making an account, but then the whole thing started feeling way stranger

the interface looks like something from a sci-fi movie. weird symbols, glowing objects, strange messages everywhere. honestly feels less like a website and more like some kind of transmission

there's also this glowing orb on the page. if you click it, it sometimes takes you somewhere else. for me it opened a youtube channel. my wife tried the exact same thing and nothing happened. no idea if it's broken, random, or intentional

then i started digging around and found some crypto token connected to it. almost nobody seems to know it exists. only a handful of wallets and barely any transactions

the weirdest part is the text. click around enough and you start getting these strange popups that read like they're written by something pretending to be human

i've spent way more time on this than i should have and i'm still not sure if it's an art project, ARG, crypto experiment, or somebody's attempt at building an alien internet

has anyone else run into this thing?

Early replies (first 3)

62379010
>>62378999
waste of a trip

it's another altcoin, but with animated backgroud and trollbox on site

62379011
>>62378999
>my wife tried the exact same thing..
This is how i know you are lying

62379076
>>62378999
Get an ad nigga

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62379143
>>62378999
uhhh, India sirs

62379147
>>62379143
kek

62379039

62379041

62379042

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62379044
>>62379035

62379046
>>62379035
If you are referring to the US, one of the reasons for this is there was a greater appetite for dollars outside the US than in it. However, things have changed since the dollar system was politicized.

62379058
I want the fed to raise rate to 35% and destroy the world.

thoughts?

62379071
>>62379041
worse, kys nigger

62379101
money exists to be spent. if you don't spend, there are no jobs, there is no development, there are no goods and services. the purpose of an economy is to produce things, not to hoard money. we can already see that the "free market", left to its own devices, prefers to hoard rather than spend, and uses hoarded resources to oppress and stifle growth rather than encourage it

item.title: World Cup is a distraction for smart money to move — 9 replies — 4.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
World Cup is a distraction for smart money to move

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-17 10:34 UTC

OP

I like this board, despite the retardness so I'll tell you sumn you already knew intuitively.
Legacy med stocks will see a 2021 spike.
(NVAX) (MRNA) (PFE) and others.
Dont you think its strange that a few months ago it seemed like the world was going to end because of Covid Cicada, Novovirus and Hantavirus outbreaks??
These stocks have been moving irregularly recently, with high volume dumps and rebuys, trying to shake off retail traders and computers.

Now here's the /x/ part.
Theyve been prepping this year, and the WC is a way to infect a significant amount of people, who will travel back to their home countries and infect others. This is what gemini said:
>World Cup Surveillance: As six million fans gather across the US, Canada, and Mexico, health officials are actively monitoring for measles (with thousands of cases already reported in the US and Mexico), dengue, hepatitis A, and mpox. Wastewater monitoring and symptom tracking are being intensified to detect outbreaks early.

You have been warned

Early replies (first 3)

62378882
>>62378876
I don't want society to collapse...

62378941
>>62378882
Get off 4chan Normie

62378943
I just need to go back to, let's say, 2013. Or even 6 months. God, I would take 6 months.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62378944
>>62378876
how will a new corona virus effect me as a neet?

62378946
>>62378944
(You) will be one of the first ones to "kill" off
I'll keep posting some more findings and ideas.

62378972
It has already pumped more then 100% in a year. Plus all the normie media shills it. Thanks, no.

62379017
>>62378876
Grill Chads cant stop winning.

62379023
>>62378876
Yeah, that sounds about right.

62379028
>>62378876
>>62378946
Other things to invest in:
>prison industrial complex
In 2020, the american prison industrial complex made 11 BILLION DOLLARS more worth of goods, off of prison labor (read slave labor)
>goytertainment
It seems that pandemics are amazing for the entertainment industry. I've noticed that the PSKY and WBD merger would become the new blackrock of streaming services.
These things are way more speculative but the post still stands

>>62378972

item.title: UNI — 7 replies — 3.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNI

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-17 10:35 UTC

OP

>Reports Say Standard Chartered Sees UNI At $100 As RWA Thesis Builds
>Uniswap’s UNI token could reach $100 by 2030, with the forecast built around a much larger market for tokenized real-world assets and on-chain trading infrastructure.
>+31% from the morning

OMG OMG OMG THIS IS BIG NEWS
Standard Chartered, guys!

https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/crypto/articles/standard-chartered-says-uni-could-231200739.html

Early replies (first 3)

62378184
+33.44%

62378187
>>62378115
Aero will kill Uni this altseason

62378197
oh yeah
what real world asset does uni tokenize

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62378211
>>62378187
>this altseason

62378221
>>62378187
>altseason
useless term
defi will win
alt L1s will die

62378991
>>62378221
Do you even know what aero is

62379030
Love Uni always have always will. Nothing will ever top the Uni airdrop and defi summer

item.title: What made you still hold BTC? — 41 replies — 33.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
What made you still hold BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-06-17 11:27 UTC

OP

Bitcoin has gone through multiple cycles, crashes, and endless criticism, yet many continue to hold. What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

Early replies (first 3)

62371733
>>62371718
>What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

62371807
The only reason to ever sell bitcoin is to buy more at a lower price.

62371917
>>62371807
correct
>waiting for a good entry

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62372396
Its not, I bought and sold like 10 times

62372419
>>62371718
I'm a bagholder since 2018. I got some returns but I'm obviously not happy. Still much better than my Link bag which is a disaster. I always notice trends too late, being hyperliquid the latest one i missed. Still bought at 62 and will try to sell at 70+.
Unironically, hype seems the only crypto i have more faith atm even though I got like 4k riding.
My BTC bag is worth 200k atm. Sucks.
Also, i started diversifying into Nasdaq and sp500 i lost faith on BTC being he fastest vehicle and i think next bull run we'll be very lucky if we even reach 180k top. We probably get front runned at 140-150k. Still I'll be dropping heavily my bags then

62372448
>>62371718
Truly decentralised money

62372468
>>62372051
The problem with NFTs is how they have such a small market cap while being extremely illiquid on top of that. Any shitcoin ranked #320 on CoinGecko has a higher market cap than Pudgy Penguins, and 50x to 100x its trading volume. That’s an automatic turn-off for any whale that would want to invest millions in the top NFTs. These bags would be hard to sell at the right moment.

62372501
>>62372380
>mfw the real Bitcoin is only $226 today

62372915
>>62372501
>>mfw the real Bitcoin is only $226 today
lol!
bcash cucks BTFO
>we don't need POW, we will have 10-block "finalisation rule!!!"
wow, just as Satoshi intended! lamao.

62373544
>>62371807
Everyone has that plan until Bitcoin decides to never give them their entry back.

62373685
>>62372056
no, they're going to print more for the US and going to bail out the cash strapped holders of US bonds with swap lines

62374097
>>62371917
What's your entry price?

62374451
>>62372043
Depends what you're optimizing for. Some people want the next 100x. Others are happy finding ways to make their BTC a bit more productive without giving up exposure entirely.

62375554
>>62372051
Maybe, but doesn't the ETF route introduce a different trade-off?

62375717
Nice triple post faglord

62376610
>>62372056
No reason to stop printing

62376672
>>62376610

62377200
>>62372468
Nft lol

62377213
>>62372915
What do you mean?

62377217
>>62374451
How do you do that?

62377221
Ethereum

62377225
>>62372419
Link is fine. Wait till $1000

62377236
>>62371718
Not a BTC investor myself, but I imagine it's hard to ignore something that has repeatedly survived the kinds of events many thought would kill it.

62377444
Nothing about bitcoin has fundamentally changed.

62377533
>>62377444
C-checked

62377691
>>62377444
>Nothing about bitcoin has fundamentally changed.
checked and confirmed

62378619
>>62377217
You can natively stake it through a protocol while still maintaining self-custody. That way, you can earn more instead of just holding your BTC.

62378621
>>62372059
Maybe he's wrong. Maybe he's right. Either way, locking in gains after buying near the lows is hard to criticize.

62378868
>>62378619
If you're doing on centralised services you're cooked

62378871
>>62377691
Genius nerd. pretentious

62378977
>>62377225
go see a doctor

62379055
>>62377221
Long or short?

62379100
>>62373685
inflation is real

item.title: I talked to two people today about rent. — 149 replies — 21.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I talked to two people today about rent.

Open thread • Replies: 149 • Updated: 2026-06-17 05:54 UTC

OP

I talked to two people today about rent.

The first guy said he lives in Atlanta and pays about $2,300 a month in rent. I mentioned that works out to roughly $27,600 a year, which I personally think is a lot for rent. He also told me he was let go from a sales job at a small company about two weeks ago. Small company = low pay. When I commented on the rent being high, he responded by saying, “maybe it is a lot for you.”

I told him it is a terrible financial decision.
I also told him that I live in a major city and my monthly taxes alone are more than that.

Later, I spoke with another guy who pays around $2,000 a month for a luxury apartment. That’s about $24,000 a year. I didn’t ask about his income or job situation, but I shared that I thought the annual rent cost seemed very high.

I kept telling him his rent was a waste of money and he should buy a house. He told me he might not want to to live somewhere for so long. I asked him how he expected to build wealth. The conversation went back and forth with me saying it was a waste of “money down the drain.” He eventually admitted it was hard and bad use of money and insulted me with “life is hard when YOU don’t live in mommies basement.”
Wow.
He is literally throwing money down the drain and I trying to give me good advice.

It made me wonder if people commonly spend over $20,000–$30,000 a year on rent? And if so, how common is that relative to income and lifestyle choices?

Please look at picrel. Do people spend so much of their paycheque on rent? How does anyone become rich?

Curious what others think about this.

Early replies (first 3)

62372611
>>62372604
>I talked to two people today about rent.
no you didn't

62372621
>$24k per year rent is throwing money down the drain
>$50k per year interest payments for 30 years to "own" the same thing is building wealth

Lol

Lmao

62372624
>>62372604

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62374657
>>62372621
What? My whole mortage is only 15k per year

62374661
>>62372604
It's very common for people to be rentslaves or house-poor their entire lives because they've been psyop shamed into avoiding living with their own family.

62376556
>>62372604
When you really think about it, home owners are losing a lot of money too. First there's the down payment, that money grows a lot faster in stocks. In some cases it may be worth more than the entire value of the house in 30 years. Housing simply can't compete with the gains from the stock market. Secondly, even if you own your home and it's a 600k asset. Living in it, is throwing money away. If you sold you could be making 60k a year from your investments. Renting for 30k a year would be a net gain.

Home owners are mostly idiots, unless they are landlords or rich and retired. It is the handicap that prevents high earners from ever making it.

62376833
>>62372604
It depends on the market of course but yeah $2300 is not high in most places these days. If you equate that to a house payment that is under $300k which in major metros is ghetto tier housing. There is nothing inherently wrong with renting from a financial standpoint, even Dave Ramsey would agree. If you are not planning to stay put for 5-10 years then you are better off renting. The transaction costs for home ownership are quite high, often 10% of the price. The most important factor is, as your picrel states, the percentage of your income you spend on housing. If you want to invest and get rich, stay under 25% of your take-home.

62376850
>>62372621
24k X infinite is more than 50k

62376855
>>62376556
Yep, people forget how much it costs to keep a home maintained. On top of taxes, interest and insurance, most houses require 1-2% of their value spent on maintenance every year. No mutual funds ever needed a new AC unit or roof.

62376874
>>62372604
Whether you are paying rent or interest, you are throwing the money away. At least with renting someone else is taking all the capital depreciation and liability risk.

62377090
>>62372604
did a retard write this shit? do you really not know the reason why people rent over buying A FUCKING HOUSE???

62377165
>>62372604
This chart is bogus. I make $80,000 a year and bought a house for $300,000 back in 2024. My monthly take home is no where near $10,000

62377214
>>62377090
Did you read the op…?

62377226
>>62376833
> is not high in most places these days. If you equate that to a house payment that is under $300k which in major metros is ghetto tier housing.
Kek>>62377165
>>62376833
> If you want to invest and get rich, stay under 25% of your take-home.
You’re not rich if you don’t own a house and everything is just tied up the market. lol.
That’s why the home ownership class in good areas are so far ahead.
Wealth gap, once again.

62377272
i own a few duplexes

every year i raise the rent and every year the tenties explain why they cant afford it

then they renew the lease anyway

its honestly kinda inspiring

62377298
Another thread where 80 IQ mongoloids with a 3% mortgage try doing the math that has already been done a million times showing that renting + equities > home ownership from a financial point of view

Btw if you stop paying property taxes the government takes your house

62377350
>>62377298
rent's due on the 1st

increasing $50

thank you for your service

62377435
>>62377350

Oh no, the landlord raised rent for the 6 month lease by 40 minutes of work a month ):

Meanwhile:

62377512
>>62372937
Huh? Who leases for more than a year? My apt has up to 15 months so I do that. But it goes up 4% every time.

I do put about $10k/yr into the market but my rent is like 50 fucking % of my after tax pay. I reconnected with my long lost dad so I have a parent with a house finally, but not sure if I'd move in just to save $25k/yr when I'll be 34 and long past should have started a family by then

62377522
>>62377435
you can show this to the leasing office when you renew

62377531
>>62372604
im sorry but every home is 300k+ and not everyone is making 10k a month.

62377592
>>62377298
invalid comparison

there's no reason you can't own a home and put just as much money into stocks.

rent isn't free. It costs as much or more than a mortgage. That's money you're throwing away

62377725
>>62372604
>mortgage is 14% of my monthly NET income.
Neighborhood is upper middle class white people and Asians.
Feels good

62377937
>>62377725
Imagine crawling into a moist bed every day. Pool rooms put off so much moisture that the humidity would make your sheets sticky every day. Not only that, but God forbid you splash and your bed is now straight up soaked. Oh yeah a and who wants to smell like pool chemicals every day? This is so retarded, but it would be fun for 45 minutes.

62377944
urbanite trash vs rural chads

62378180
>>62377937
I believe that was just a staged photo anon, chill.

62378190
Hmmmm
>rent cheap with enough spare income to invest in the stock market
Or
>pay an expensive mortgage with Insurance just to be house broke and own the property in 30 years

Ownership is good for the sake of owning but it is such a shitty investment vehicle.

62378268
>>62372604
Look man tell me I'm wrong or that I'm coping or that I'm stupid I don't care what you say to me but this shit genuinely sounds like it didn't happen. I don't know why, but whenever you make these threads about you speaking to people or the gym people going home to count their money it just comes off as a made up scenario that only happened inside your head. I don't know what you're trying to do or if you need meds or maybe I need meds but holy shit it just reads like cringe imaginary scenarios. Maybe you're just autistic and need some form of outlet or something...

62378361
>>62372604
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62378368
>>62372604
>Small company = low pay
What?

62378369
>>62378180
IDK, there was a lot of crazy shit in the 70s.

>>62378268
Definitely never happened, can't care what OP says because they deny it every time

62378396
>get mortgage
>miss a payment by a picosecond on your last month
>lose the house and 20 years of your life
Maybe if we lived in an era where you could keep the same job forever, i would not be afraid, but nowadays you are gambling on the grueling torture of the job market after an unavoidable layoff

62378653
>>62372937
LOL delusional. People with mortgages before 2021 are paying less than renters with lower loan amounts and 2% interest rate, a modern aristocracy if you will. Renters live at the mercy of their landlords
